description_long: This is a metallic L-shaped hex key, commonly known as an Allen wrench, with a uniform hexagonal cross-section along its entire length. The tool is made from a single piece of solid metal, likely steel, and features a matte, slightly reflective finish. Both arms of the L are straight and of different lengths, allowing for leverage and access in tight spaces. The ends are flat and precisely cut, designed to fit snugly into hexagonal socket screws. The surface appears smooth with no visible grip pattern or additional coatings. There are no visible markings, logos, or signs of wear, giving it a clean and utilitarian appearance.
description: A metallic L-shaped hex key (Allen wrench) with a smooth, matte finish and hexagonal cross-section, designed for driving hex socket screws.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full L-shape and hexagonal profile of the wrench.
description_view_1: End-on view highlighting the hexagonal cross-section and the thickness of the metal.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, mirroring view 0, showing the L-shape and straight arms.
description_view_3: Edge-on view displaying the narrow profile and length of the wrench.
